summarization.a: CH₃ coverage set at half of the optimum level, resulting in a lower growth rate of 0.8 Å/cycle, while OH coverage remains unchanged, illustrating the effect of reduced CH₃ coverage on ALD growth.
summarization.b: H (or OH) coverage at half the optimum level, leading to a non-linear reduction in growth rate to 1.1 Å/cycle and a surface saturated with a mixture of Al(CH₃) and Al(CH₃)₂, illustrating the effect of reduced H/OH coverage on ALD kinetics.

data_extraction.a:
| Species | Coverage | Growth Rate (Å/cycle) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| [CH₃] | 0.5 × optimum | 0.8 | Reduced TMA adsorption lowers growth rate |
| OH | optimum | – | Hydroxyl coverage unchanged |
data_extraction.b:
| Species | Coverage | Growth Rate (Å/cycle) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| H | 0.5 × optimum | 1.1 | Partial dehydroxylation affects growth rate non-linearly |
| Al(CH₃)/Al(CH₃)₂ | mixed | – | Surface saturated mixture at end of TMA pulse |

a.question: Does reducing CH₃ coverage to half of the optimum level affect the growth rate of alumina during ALD?

b.question: How does the growth rate change when H (or OH) coverage is reduced to half of the optimum compared to the reduction in CH₃ coverage?
b.answer: With H (or OH) coverage at half the optimum, the growth rate decreases to 1.1 Å/cycle, which is less than the drop observed with half CH₃ coverage (0.8 Å/cycle).

d.question: Explain how deviations from optimum surface coverage influence ALD kinetics and film
d.answer: Deviations from optimum surface coverage significantly affect ALD kinetics. Lower CH₃ coverage limits the number of TMA molecules that can adsorb, directly reducing the growth rate. Reduced H or OH coverage alters the surface termination non-linearly, partially limiting reactions during the H₂O pulse and producing a mixed surface of Al(CH₃) and Al(CH₃)₂. These effects result in slower growth and highlight the importance of maintaining sufficient surface coverage for efficient and predictable ALD film deposition.

c.question: List the surface species shown in the reaction portraits of Figure 5 and their coverage effects.
c.answer: [CH₃] – half optimum in 5a, reduces growth rate, OH – unchanged in 5a, H – half optimum in 5b, affects growth rate non-linearly, Al(CH₃)/Al(CH₃)₂ – surface mixture in 5b
